person.name: Kasun Hapangama
person.description: Software Developer with 3+ years building secure APIs and enterprise web applications in Java and .NET. Contracts through SoftSora for Japanese enterprise clients, and leads a software engineering team at SUNDEVS building commercial developer tools.

summary.headline: I build secure APIs and enterprise systems in Java & .NET for clients across Japan, and lead the engineering team at SUNDEVS.
summary.about: I'm a software developer with 3+ years building secure APIs and enterprise systems in Java and .NET. I founded SUNDEVS and lead the engineering team shipping our commercial products, and contract through SoftSora for clients across Japan. Currently studying Software Engineering at SLIIT. I care about systems that are fast, dependable, and genuinely useful.

responsibilities: Full-stack development across React, Next.js, Node.js, Nest.js, Express.js, Spring Boot and .NET.
Own software testing, project management, documentation, and stakeholder collaboration.
Run DevOps on AWS and Oracle Cloud, from CI/CD to deployment and monitoring.
Drive AI-assisted automation and internal tooling for client sales workflows.
Designed and built features for the car-marketplace project using Spring Framework and .NET.
Worked directly with the Japanese client on requirements, documentation, and delivery.
Handled software project management and customer collaboration.
Built secure merchant-registration APIs for the car-marketplace project.
Founded and run SUNDEVS, building and selling commercial developer tools.
Lead a software engineering team to build our products.
500+ copies sold across commercial developer tools.
Built custom server-side addons and an event-management system for Spigot servers.

projects.name: Car Marketplace Buyer Registration Platform
projects.meta: 2024 · SoftSora · Client: a Japanese car marketplace (Osaka, Japan)
projects.summary: Replaced a manual Excel/email buyer-registration process for a Japanese car-marketplace with a self-service web platform: registration, car-matching preferences, a personal dashboard, and match notifications.
problem: The client runs a car bulk-appraisal service connecting vehicle sellers with buyers across Japan.
Buyer registration was handled entirely by hand with Excel sheets and email.
Slow, error-prone, and not scalable as the buyer base grew.
solution: Built a web app to automate and streamline buyer onboarding.
User registration with personal and contact details.
Car-matching condition registration (make, model, year, price range).
Personalized dashboard to view preferences, track seller interactions, and manage the account.
Automated notifications about new listings matching a buyer's criteria.
result: 1,000+ buyers registered and onboarded on the platform.
Replaced manual Excel and email work with a self-service flow.
Ongoing project with continuous improvements.

projects.name: Car Marketplace Vehicle Data Platform
projects.meta: 2024 · SoftSora · Client: a Japanese car marketplace
projects.summary: Re-architected a static, hard-to-update vehicle dataset into a dynamic model on S3 + CloudFront with an EC2 microservice, while keeping the pre-loaded speed.
projects.highlight: Publishing time cut from days to hours; sub-1ms reads.
problem: The existing system used static JSON files embedded directly in the frontend.
Adding or updating vehicle data required manual code changes.
Publishing new data took 1 to 2 days.
solution: Introduced a dynamic vehicle-data model using JSON files on S3 served via CloudFront.
A separate microservice on EC2 to create, update, and remove vehicle data.
Kept the static, pre-loaded delivery model so reads stay extremely fast.
result: Cut data publishing time from 1 to 2 days down to a few hours.
Sub-1ms data access after initial load, faster than typical API-based systems.
More scalable and far easier to maintain.

projects.name: AI-Powered Sales Proposal Engine
projects.meta: 2026 · SoftSora · Client: a marketing agency, Japan
projects.summary: An engine that reads a client's own records plus dozens of public signals and generates timely, context-aware proposals tuned to the local Japanese market and industry.
projects.highlight: Hour-long research reduced to seconds, consistently.
problem: The sales team manually researched every prospect (site speed, reviews, social media, renewal timing) before pitching.
Slow, inconsistent, and dependent on whoever was working; good leads went cold.
No single place combining known customer data with real-world signals.
solution: Built an AI engine that researches and reasons, then hands over a ready-to-use proposal.
Reads the client's own records (contracts, plans, payment history) to spot renewal windows, service gaps, and consolidation opportunities.
Reads public signals (slow or insecure sites, stale footers, quiet social accounts, recurring review complaints, competitor ranking, hiring, seasonal timing) into plain-language insights.
Tunes each suggestion to the local context and industry language.
result: Hour-long research now happens in seconds, the same way every time.
Consistent proposal quality regardless of who is at the desk.
Renewal windows and openings surfaced automatically instead of missed.

projects.name: CRM Unification & AI Enrichment
projects.meta: 2026 · SoftSora · Client: a B2B marketing agency (Japan)
projects.summary: Merged three disconnected contact sources into one CRM, enriched ~6,000 bare records, and normalized 79 job-title variations to auto-sort decision-makers.
problem: Contacts were split across three tools (CRM, a business-card app, Excel) and never joined up.
Thousands of older records lacked business details; no way to tell decision-makers from junior staff.
The job-title field was a mess: one role written 79 different ways.
solution: Built one unified pipeline so business-card contacts import the same clean way as event contacts, with duplicates merged by email.
Full clean-up and enrichment, auto-filling missing details (website, company info) across ~6,000 contacts.
Normalized 79 title variations into standard categories and auto-sorted decision-makers vs. others.
result: One source of truth across all channels.
3,000+ genuine decision-makers cleanly identified out of ~6,700 contacts.
Targeting in seconds; ~6,000 records enriched and normalized at scale.

projects.name: HAG: Human Approval Gateway
projects.summary: A self-hosted gateway between systems that need approval and the people who give it. Posts interactive Approve/Reject messages to Slack, applies a rule, and notifies the source system.
projects.highlight: Approvals from minutes to seconds; fully audited.
problem: Getting human approval for sensitive operations (production deploys, high-value transactions) was a bottleneck.
Approvals were lost across emails, Slack, and ticketing systems: fragmented, slow, error-prone.
solution: Built HAG, a self-hosted gateway between systems that need approval and the people who give it.
Listens for requests from any system, posts interactive Approve/Reject messages to Slack, applies a rule (first-response-wins or all-must-agree), and notifies the originating system.
Works with anything that can make an HTTP request.
result: Approvals go from minutes (hunting people down) to seconds.
Every decision logged, audited, and visible; no more lost or missed approvals.

projects.name: OPProtector
projects.summary: A real-time security plugin that monitors Minecraft operator accounts for compromise and unauthorized access, alerting admins before damage is done.
problem: Minecraft server admins faced unauthorized access to operator accounts; a single compromise grants full server control.
No automated way to detect operator abuse; admins relied on manual log parsing and reactive responses.
solution: A security plugin that scans and monitors operator accounts in real time.
Continuous monitoring, automated detection of blacklisted permissions and unauthorized patterns, real-time admin alerts, and early preventive action.
result: 19,000+ servers protected (and counting); adopted across thousands of servers.
75+ GitHub stars; zero false positives via intelligent detection; immediate threat response.

projects.name: Sales Research Pipeline
projects.summary: One click after an appointment is confirmed generates a full research pack (company, website, and Google Maps audit), filed and delivered before the meeting.
problem: Before every sales meeting, reps manually researched each prospect: company, online presence, Google Maps profile, background.
Doing this by hand was slow, inconsistent, and often skipped when busy.
solution: Built an automated pipeline triggered the moment an appointment is confirmed.
Researches the company and market, analyses the website, and audits the Google Maps presence.
Generates three ready-to-read reports, files them, and notifies the sales team.
result: Reps walk into every meeting fully prepared with zero extra effort.
Research that took time per prospect now happens instantly and consistently.
Handles large batches at once and self-corrects on errors.

projects.name: Hidden Sales Leads on Autopilot
projects.summary: A daily engine that scrapes job sites, normalizes messy Japanese company names, dedupes against the client DB, and files genuinely new leads automatically.
problem: A sales team relied on a large internal company database to find new business.
Someone manually visited job sites, copied company names, and checked each against the database.
Japanese company names vary widely (abbreviations, spellings), making matching hard; an earlier automation attempt hit tool time-limits.
solution: Built a daily automated system that visits major job sites, gathers company names, and normalizes spelling variations into one entity.
Checks each company against the internal database; genuinely new ones are added as fresh leads with a summary to the team.
Delivered in two stages: a reviewable spreadsheet first to build trust, then full integration into the client's tools.
result: Moved from a fragile, person-dependent routine to a dependable daily lead engine.
More thorough coverage; the messy company-name problem handled automatically.
Designed to grow; new sources can be added without a rebuild.

projects.name: Weekly Deal Summaries
projects.summary: Every Monday, deals closing that week are pulled from HubSpot and posted to Google Chat — full automation despite a no-automation HubSpot plan.
problem: All deal data lived in HubSpot, but their plan had no automation features.
Staying on top of closing deals meant logging in and pulling numbers by hand every week.
Data lived in HubSpot while the team communicated in Google Chat, with no bridge between them.
solution: Built an automated weekly digest connecting HubSpot and Google Chat.
Every Monday 9:00 AM it finds deals closing that week and posts a tidy summary (name, stage, amount, close date) into the team's Google Chat space.
Used a separate automation platform as the bridge, no HubSpot upgrade needed; prototyped first, then moved to production.
result: The team starts each week knowing exactly where to focus, with zero manual effort.
Fewer missed deals; a manual chore became fully hands-off.

projects.name: SunDevs Dashboard
projects.summary: A unified customer platform (licenses, servers, requests, scheduling, and a 20-state support chatbot) that shifted support from reactive to 24/7 self-service.
problem: SunDevs customers had no centralized way to manage assets, track service requests, or get support.
Support teams were overwhelmed with repetitive questions; the experience was fragmented across systems.
solution: Built a unified, web-based customer management platform: a single source of truth.
Real-time dashboard for licenses, servers, and requests with interactive charts and light/dark themes.
Self-service asset management: licenses, server monitoring, service requests, and call scheduling.
Selection-based support chatbot (no typing) for purchases, docs, requests, and scheduling, with anti-duplicate and rate-limit safeguards.
Role-based access (Customer/Admin), Discord OAuth2, audit logging, and email verification.
result: Shifted customer experience from reactive (waiting for support) to proactive (24/7 self-service).
Dramatically reduced support workload; scales without proportional staffing.
2,857+ lines of documentation; chatbot state machine with 20+ states and 11 conversation flows.

projects.name: Discord Leaderboards
projects.summary: Live Minecraft leaderboards in Discord via PlaceholderAPI & webhooks. First commercial product and the foundation for starting SunDevs.
projects.highlight: 40+ GitHub stars. Adopted across hundreds of servers.
problem: Server owners wanted to showcase top players in their Discord communities in real time.
No simple way to extract leaderboard data and display it in Discord without custom integrations.
solution: A lightweight plugin connecting Minecraft server data directly to Discord.
Pulls rankings via PlaceholderAPI, posts via webhooks or DiscordSRV slash commands, updates on schedule, and supports unlimited custom leaderboards across MC 1.8.8 to 1.19.4.
result: Adopted across hundreds of servers and thousands of players.
40+ GitHub stars; released on SpigotMC, BuiltByBit, and PolyMart; open-source (MIT) with a premium version.
My first commercial product, and the foundation for starting SunDevs.

projects.name: SummitEvents
projects.summary: A config-driven event system for Minecraft servers: live leaderboards, boss-bar HUD, and automated rewards for timed competitive events.
problem: Server admins needed to run time-limited competitive events (mining/farming challenges) but coordinating them manually was tedious and error-prone.
No straightforward way to launch scheduled events, track rankings live, auto-reward winners, or show progress and time.
solution: Built a comprehensive event management plugin with event creation and configuration.
Real-time leaderboards, automated broadcast messaging, and a boss-bar HUD (score, position, time remaining).
Automated reward commands and flexible event types (block breaking, farming) with customizable materials.
result: Made events easy to organize and more engaging with live progress tracking and automated recognition.
Config-driven design let the client launch diverse event types without code changes.

products.name: SunLicense
products.description: Create, validate, and control software license keys, with a Java API, Discord bot, and docs.

products.name: SunGuard
products.description: Wraps ProGuard & yGuard to make Java code unreadable, from one simple dashboard.

products.name: SunPaste
products.description: Self-hosted paste sharing with access controls, a REST API, and an admin panel.

products.name: SunLinks
products.description: Self-hostable URL shortener with your own domain, click tracking, and analytics.
